description: Run a generated CE spec scan for schemas where properties.data is exactly {} and record component names, operation counts, and examples.
Run the same untyped-data scan against the generated EE spec and verify counts match expectations or document EE-only differences.

After fixing clientsContacts.ts, inspect GET /api/v1/clients/{id} in CE JSON and verify the 200 response data property resolves to typed client fields such as client_id, client_name, billing_cycle, tenant, account_manager_full_name, and logoUrl.
After fixing clientsContacts.ts, inspect GET /api/v1/clients/{id} in EE JSON and verify it has the same typed client response envelope.
For each replaced generic success component, sample at least one list, one detail, and one mutation operation when present and verify response data is typed appropriately.
Run npm --prefix sdk run openapi:generate and verify CE/legacy specs regenerate successfully.
Run npm --prefix sdk run openapi:generate -- --edition ee and verify EE specs regenerate successfully.
Run CE and EE placeholder scans and verify both remain at zero placeholder operations.
Run npm run guard:ext-v2 and verify no guarded extension-v2 source literals are introduced.
Run the final untyped-data scan and verify no success response data schemas remain as {}, except explicitly documented allowlist entries if any.
Before each commit, run git status --short and verify .env.localtest remains unstaged.
At completion, update PR notes or summary with before/after untyped-data counts, placeholder counts, generation commands, and guard results.
